responseMessageContents.text: The user wants to know the Git repository link for this project so they can use Claude Code to make changes to the code. Let me look at the git configuration to find the remote URL.

responseMessageContents.text: The git remote is configured to a Rork-managed URL. Let me get the actual URL that the user could use to clone this repo. Let me check the git config more carefully.

The user wants to be able to clone this repo and work on it with Claude Code. The Git remote is a Rork-managed URL that uses a special authentication. Let me check if there's a more standard Git URL or if the user needs to know the project ID to connect.

Let me check if Rork has any way to expose the repo URL for external access.

The Git remote for this project is:

```
https://rork-git-router.rork-direct.workers.dev/git/d63xpwoiquo4ov1vndqwf
```

That URL is the raw repo endpoint, but it uses Rork-managed authentication (the `git:e2b-egress-managed@` credentials are automatically injected in the sandbox). You won't be able to clone it with a personal Git client or Claude Code using that URL directly — the auth is scoped to Rork's environment.

The project ID is **`d63xpwoiquo4ov1vndqwf`** — that's the key piece. If there's a way to expose the repo through a Rork CLI or API for external use, that would be the path. Unfortunately, I don't see a direct mechanism here for a standalone clone + push workflow. You'd need to either:

- Work entirely inside Rork (which syncs everything automatically), or
- Clone the files locally from the Rork workspace and set up a separate repo from there

Is there something specific you're trying to do with Claude Code that I can help with instead?
